rxn_class_summary: EPR spin-trapping experiments using DMPO to detect carbonate radical (CO3•−) and peroxycarbonate radical (CO4•−) intermediates generated in the electrochemical/catalytic system.
electrochemical_role: DMPO spin trap captures reactive oxygen-centered radicals (CO3•− and CO4•−) generated in the system; the radicals are presumed to form via oxidation of (bi)carbonate/peroxycarbonate species, enabling EPR detection of the radical intermediates.

key_intermediates.name: DMPO-OCO2 adduct (carbonate addition to DMPO nitrone carbon)
DMPO-OH adduct (final EPR-active species after hydrolysis)
DMPO-OOH adduct (final EPR-active species after hydrolysis)

electron_flow: CO3•− or CO4•− adds to the α-C of the DMPO nitrone to give a transient DMPO-OCO2 or DMPO-OOCO2 adduct; subsequent hydrolysis releases HCO3− and yields the EPR-detectable DMPO-OH or DMPO-OOH spin adduct.

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: Forms DMPO-OH adduct from CO3•− and DMPO-OOH adduct from CO4•−, both detected by EPR, confirming the presence of carbonate and peroxycarbonate radicals.

notes: The DMPO-OH and DMPO-OOH adducts observed by EPR are commonly attributed to •OH and •OOH directly; here the authors propose they arise via nucleophilic addition of CO3•−/CO4•− followed by hydrolytic loss of HCO3−, an alternative interpretation that should be considered when assigning EPR signals.
